enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on June 17, 2018, recapping a solo bike ride the previous day north toward Lyons via Apple Valley Road and Highway 36, which was cut short by rain and a dead Garmin. He talks about his low fitness/endurance, hanging out with his mom to look at washer-dryers and doors at Home Depot, trouble sleeping despite being exhausted, cleaning his house, and the death of a pet fish he'd had since 2013. He muses about wanting to become a triathlete to stay fit.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: His endurance is at its worst ever and he believes his heart has atrophied from inactivity; expects about a month to recover
Wants to become a triathlete because it's the best way to stay fit year-round
Open to it — has ridden in worse conditions (e.g., Poland in 2010) and thinks you just get used to it

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, I thought my garment was charged, but it wasn't because it died halfway through my ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My garment doesn't connect to my computer, so there's nothing I can do with it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hit start on my ride because I was high and thought it would play all the way.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't have proof of my ride because I didn't record it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, I cleaned and organized my house but still have a lot to do.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode up north to Alliance but didn't go to the mountains because it was raining.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took Apple Valley Road until it turned to dirt, then turned around because it started raining.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My endurance is really bad, worse than it's ever been before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think my heart has atrophied from lack of exercise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe it will take about a month for my heart to regain enough size and strength to ride every day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After my ride, I hung out with my mom and we went to get a meal together.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We went to Home Depot to look at washer and dryer sets because I want to do laundry all the time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My mom shopped for doors and ordered a new sliding door.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's been raining and drizzling outside.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When I'm tired, I don't know a lot, and when I'm not tired, I don't know what I'm saying to others.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My brain power is low because I'm tired and not healthy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I yawn a lot to get more blood to my brain, but only when doing videos, not when riding my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had trouble falling asleep last night and was just lying there until morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After my ride, I knew I needed rest but not necessarily sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to get to a point where rest and sleep feel the same.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was cleaning my toilet yesterday and feel like I can do anything once I finish.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a fish that died after I put the light back on in its tank.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The fish had been alive since 2013, about five years, and I think the light killed it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to get a bunch of little fish to replace the one that died.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My goal is to become an athlete and get in shape.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think being a triathlete is the best way to stay fit because it keeps me active.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might stay inside and run on a treadmill or swim if the weather is bad.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have ridden in worse weather, like torrential rain in Poland in 2010.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The rain is pretty and you won't get burnt, but it's new to me.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-log for the day of June 17th, 2018.
So, I'll tell you what's been going on.
So, yesterday, I think after I did the V-log, I went on a ride.
But my Garmin, I thought my Garmin was charged, but it wasn't because it died.
Like, halfway through it.
Oh, that reminded me.
I don't think it even, yeah, it doesn't even connect to my computer anyway.
So, there's nothing I can do with it.
And when I went on the ride, I hit start.
Because I think I was just doing fanciful thinking.
It was really high.
And I was like, it'll work.
So, I didn't try to do it with my cell phone.
So, I had no proof of my ride yesterday.
But, oh, yeah, I'll do it with my cell phone today.
Anyway, so yesterday, I got up and I cleaned my house.
Some more, I'm organized some more in my house, you know.
And I have a lot of that to do still.
I'm way behind on that.
But then I rode up north to Lions.
I wanted to go in the mountains, but it looked like it was raining.
So, then I kept going up.
And then there's Apple Valley Road.
And I took a right up a couple miles until it turned dirt and turned around.
And then it started raining.
And I came down Apple Valley Road.
And then came down Highway 36 and took a left on St. Brayne.
And then I ride on 75th.
And then came back, like, at the end of that road before hitting the diagonal.
So, I don't know.
That was like three hours or something.
I didn't ride the day before, but I was really beat yesterday.
My endurance is really bad.
Like, yeah, a whole level worse than I've ever seen it before.
I think it's just been so long since I've exercised that I think my heart atrophied.
And I think it'll just take a little bit of time, like a month, for my heart to gain enough size and strength to be able to, like, ride every day.
So, yesterday when I came back from my ride, I went in to hung out with my mom.
She came over and we went to errands together.
We went to, got a meal and then went to Home Depot.
And we're looking at washer-dryer sets I want to get.
I'm going to start riding my bike all the time.
I want to do laundry all the time.
And, um, and, uh, then she went shopping for doors.
And, um, um, yeah, ordered some door, like a new sliding door.
And, uh, it smells nice.
It's been raining.
Look at it outside.
It's, like, drizzling right now, it seems like.
So, um, I'm going to have to sit here and be like, okay, like, what am I going to do today?
So, um, and then what else?
Uh, there was another error, but I can't, I'm yawning a lot right now, so that must mean that I'm tired.
Um, when I'm tired, I yawn a lot.
And when I'm not tired, I don't yawn.
I'm the same as other people.
It's just that I yawn more.
Um, so, yeah, I went, I came back, and I, uh, was trying to, uh, clean before I was hanging out with her, but not after.
I was, I think I was just, it was late in the day, and I was tired, and I can't even remember what I did.
My, my brain power is so low, because I'm tired.
Also, because I was, I was, like, not healthy, you know?
And then I think my brain power was going down, and I'm, yeah, so now exercising all the time.
It's, it's, like, like, all these, all the yawning, I guess, to get the blood to my brain.
Um, so, uh, luckily I don't yawn like this when I'm riding my bike, only when I'm doing videos.
That'd be annoying if I was not yawning like that all day long, I imagine.
Um, so, uh, I, uh, yeah, I went to, I slept in bed, but I, I got in bed, but I, uh, I, uh, it took me a while to get to sleep again.
I, uh, I was just laying there, like, until, like, um, three in the morning, you know?
Um, but I, but ever since the end of my ride, I knew that I definitely didn't want to get any more exercise, you know?
Like, I needed rest, but I didn't need sleep.
Because what I'm used to, because, like, from when I'm young and fit, needing rest and needing sleep are the same thing, you know?
Like, like, you can get back from a ride, and then you can continue your day, no problem, you know?
You, you could stretch if you wanted to.
But yesterday I come back, and I couldn't stretch because I didn't have energy, I needed rest.
But I still couldn't sleep, so.
I'd like to, uh, get it to the, uh, point where it's, um, it's the same thing.
Or at least when I'm getting rest, I can do other things, you know?
Like, thinking not to work on my computer.
I can clean. I was cleaning yesterday.
I, I'm still scrubbing my toilet.
After I'm done with that, I don't feel like I can do anything.
And then I'll, I'll just, uh, yeah, take it from there and keep scrubbing.
I don't know if I said, but I put my hamster in my little, um, my big garden.
I thought he'd like it in there, but then he got away.
And he was missing for a couple days, but we found him yesterday, and my fish died.
I had a fish that I had ever since I moved to Boulder, like, in 2013, like, five years old.
And, and then the light died, and then I put the light back on, like, a couple days ago.
And I think the light killed him.
Um, so I should have kept the light off.
It was keeping him alive.
So, too late now.
I'll have to replace him with a bunch of little ones.
So, yeah, I don't really, I honestly don't know what I'm going to do today.
My, my goal, I want to be, uh, athlete.
I want to get in shape.
And, um, um, I think the best way, I want to be a triathlete, because, um, I can't.
Um, it's the best way, because it keeps you, it's the best, I think it's easiest way to keep you fit.
Because days like this, I might want to just stay inside and do something else, like run on a treadmill or swim or something.
Um, but, uh, but, uh, maybe I'll walk around outside and see if it's warm enough for me to ride.
I mean, I do have the clothing for it.
I've ridden in stuff a lot worse than this.
I remember in 2010, I was biking through Europe, and I was riding through Poland, and, and, like, torrential rain, storms that were freezing.
And, um, you know, it was alright.
You just get used to it.
So, and it is kind of pretty in its own little way, you know.
You're not going to get burnt.
So anyways, it's just so new to me.
I have to think about it.
I'm like, who lives?
Okay.
Alrighty, peace out.
